description: Beverages and drinkable comforts found across settings.
foods: Tea
Steeped until it turns the color of amber, poured into a cup that warms both hands at once.
Cider
Pressed from autumn apples, sweet enough to drink cold and warm enough to drink hot.
Milk
Fresh and cool, still tasting faintly of the morning and the meadow where it came from.
Broth
Clear and golden, sipped from a mug when everything else feels like too much.
Berry Juice
Dark and tart, staining lips and fingers and the front of anyone's shirt who drinks it carelessly.
Honeyed Water
Plain water stirred with a spoonful of honey, simple enough for anyone and sweet enough for everyone.
Mint Water
Cold water with crushed mint leaves drifting in it, sharp and bright and good for hot days.
Warm Cocoa
Rich and dark and poured too hot, the kind of drink that makes you sit down and stay.
Ginger Brew
Spicy and fizzing slightly, it bites the tongue and warms the chest on the way down.
Lemonade
Sour and sweet in equal measure, the drink of long afternoons and nothing to do but sit.
